verdict: Strong batch. All three posts have a clear argument grounded in Moesta's framework with concrete VRM application. The newsletter synthesizes the week and adds meaningful new material on hire/fire and onboarding structure that doesn't appear in the posts. Voice is consistent and operator-appropriate throughout.
strengths: Post 1 (Four Forces) is immediately actionable. The habit vs. anxiety diagnostic distinction is a real improvement on how most operators think about stalled deals. Scripts for both response types are conversational and specific.

Post 2 (Struggling Moment) correctly identifies the single best question in the listing appointment and shows how to use the answer. The owner-type field note at the end adds specificity without overloading the post.
Post 3 (Demand-Side vs Supply-Side) is the sharpest conceptual frame of the three. The investor pushback field note at the end demonstrates nuance rather than oversimplification.

Newsletter adds genuine new material: the 3-step onboarding sequence and the passive search concept are not repeated from the posts. The hire/fire application to churn is specific enough to be immediately usable.
No em dashes. CTA footer present and intact on all three posts. Header formatting correct throughout.
problems: Post 1 runs slightly long — the body content is detailed. Should verify character count before publishing.
Post 2 uses 'Most VRM operators' twice in the opening section. Soften the second instance per voice rules.
Newsletter 'NEXT WEEK' teaser reveals the next book by title, which is consistent with the series format but should be confirmed as intended.
required_fixes: Verify Post 1 character count — trim body if over 2,900 characters.
Soften second categorical 'Most VRM operators' in Post 2 to 'Many VRM operators' or restructure.
Confirm 'NEXT WEEK' teaser in newsletter naming Building a StoryBrand is intentional.
